steps.title: Use corresponding angles
steps.content: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are congruent. Since $N$ corresponds to $R$, angle $R$ has the same measure as angle $N$.

steps.title: Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent
steps.content: Since triangle $LMN$ is similar to triangle $PQR$ with the correspondence $N \leftrightarrow R$, we apply the fundamental property of similar figures: corresponding angles are congruent. Therefore, $\angle R = \angle N$.

steps.title: Substitute the given angle measure
steps.content:
We're given that $\angle N = 24^{\circ}$, so $\angle R = 24^{\circ}$.

Note: The condition $3LM = PQ$ specifies that side lengths are in a ratio of $1:3$ (the scale factor), but angles in similar triangles are always equal regardless of size. The scale factor affects side lengths and areas, not angles.

C is correct.
